title: Evangelion and the Female gender
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion and the Female gender             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Tue Jan 22, 2008 2:41 pm This is something that has been on my mind for a while now, so I thought I'd bring it up to spark some conversation. Do forgive if this has been brought up before. I'm sure many of you have noticed that the female gender is given a position of very high dominance in NGE. A cursory look at some of the primary principals and themes of the show reinforce this: -Lilith and Adam, the two SoLs - godlike beings - are (likely at the very least) female. -It is implied that the angels could all be female. -The Evas themselves are female, at least so far as they contain only female souls. -Gendo's entire motivation throughout the series is to reunite with his beloved wife, whom he is clearly obsessed with. -In addition, he chose to take on her last name, and even passed that along to his son. -The climax of the series features a giant naked goddess-like figure. -Of the pilots, two of the three major children are female. Any other included male pilots are promptly renered dead or useless. -Females are placed in positions of very high authority (especially Ritsuko and her mother Aoko). -The pilots must synch up with their mother's resident soul via the A10 nerve - but only with their own mothers. -The series is brimming with subtle and not-in-the-least-bit-subtle imagery of female genetalia or breasts, and not (usually) in the typical fan-servicey way. Now don't get me wrong, my intent here is not to imply that women can't have a high rank or authority - far from it. It's a little refreshing in some cases, like in Misato's respectable military rank or Ritsuko's vast responsibilities and knowlege. But I am wondering - has anyone noticed this but me? Does anyone know what was the motivation behind the choice to place these women and the female gender in particular in a very much center-stage role? What's with the imagery? Is it all just to conjure up infantile memories of the viewer's mother? Is it sexual? Is there anything from Anno on why this is, or are we all left to speculate as per his usual?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jan 22, 2008 7:54 pm Well, Anno did pick Nadia to be the heroine in Blue Water, and Gunbuster was also about girls (Dunno that much about the more recent Gainax works). Yes, this has been talked about a long, long time. Females are highly prominent in NGE, but considering the influence of other male characters (Shinji, Gendo and Chairman Keel), there is a certain level of balance across the board. If nothing else, we notice the female prominence in NGE because a large number of anime are skewed towards the male side.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jan 22, 2008 7:55 pm Anno's a Perv, 'nuff said. You can cross off a few of those too. -Lilith and Adam, the two SoLs - godlike beings - are (likely at the very least) female. -The climax of the series features a giant naked goddess-like figure.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Tue Jan 22, 2008 8:34 pm Yes, I've heard that all the Angels are female. But just because Shamshel looks like a penis (?) doesn't mean it's male. Could be just how the animators wanted the design to be. And from most of the anime I've seen, the plots are usually based on females or female-related things, so it's not that surprising.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jan 22, 2008 8:46 pm The Angel genders are not official. And there's really no "solid" way of proving them one way or another. http://evageeks.org/FGP/Gender#ANGELS

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jan 23, 2008 2:28 am Copy-pasta about the series being a standard super-robot with the sexes swapped aside, the you don't have to squint too hard to see the set-up as a harem anime, with an even more useless protagonist than usual. Sachiel_13 wrote: Anno's a perv. Lot's of artists are.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Jan 23, 2008 7:02 am One silent, stoic girl with a hint of motherliness, one abrasive redhead girl with some sort of crush on him, one drunken slob/soldier who would rather go to bed with any cute boy...hardly what I'd call harem.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Jan 23, 2008 10:43 am @ Sachiel: It's a bit shallow to simply ascribe it to Anno being a perv. Granted, yes, he was WAS a former otaku, and otaku tend to be rather pervy. I personally think part of it was Anno reacting against, as someone else stated earlier, how most anime was often male-dominated. You see this in many Miyazaki films as well. Also, Picasso and hentai are not even on the same level.

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Wed Jan 23, 2008 5:34 pm UrsusArctos wrote: One silent, stoic girl with a hint of motherliness, one abrasive redhead girl with some sort of crush on him, one drunken slob/soldier who would rather go to bed with any cute boy...hardly what I'd call harem.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jan 23, 2008 6:02 pm chee wrote: It's a bit shallow to simply ascribe it to Anno being a perv. Also, Picasso and hentai are not even on the same level.

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Thu Jan 24, 2008 12:07 pm Wow, so many responses. I was busy yesterday so forgive the late replies. I think I should have been more specific, and for that I appologize. I was kinda wondering if there was something more metaphorical going on. It seems almost to harken back to a kind of goddess worship (especially in the case of GNR). I have to admit that I'm not the most up-to-speed on Anno's other works. I've never seen Nadia nor Gunbuster, so I had no real frame of reference as far as that goes. So are we all saying that it's really just that Anno and GAINAX decided to do... well, basically just the opposite of what virtually every other Mech anime has ever done? I was kinda hoping there was more to it than that. /disappointment.

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Thu Jan 24, 2008 12:13 pm Double-post appologies, but another thing as well: Quote: -The series is brimming with subtle and not-in-the-least-bit-subtle imagery of female genetalia or breasts, and not (usually) in the typical fan-servicey way. Anno's a perv. Lot's of artists are. Have you seen Picasso? And Hentai...

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Jan 24, 2008 3:44 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: If it were all men, it would be boring.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Jan 24, 2008 3:50 pm It's not really all that uncommon. Gods/Goddesses and sexual imagery in fiction and, in particularly mythology similar to NGE is kinda a major staple. That said, it's also quite logical in terms of NGE's themes. When the story is centered on a young man coming into adulthood, this kind of stuff only makes sense as that's really the age when you're torn between what came before (mother is the first other) and where you should be going.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 24, 2008 4:33 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: What about Armisael?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Jan 24, 2008 8:33 pm D'oh! Well, I guess that's right.

[]:             Quote Post by shito » Fri Jan 25, 2008 9:29 am If the Evas are female because they have a female soul, then that contradicts Adam being female, since it's sould is in a male body. I always wondered where do people see the Evas feminine. They look very manly, with no breasts or curves. Anyway, since Adam and Lilith are indeed seeds of life and can generate life forms themselves, I don't think they are male or female.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jan 25, 2008 9:50 am The Kabbalistic view of God was of an Androgynous being, both male and female. And Evas don't have breasts? Well, a lot of lifeforms don't...only mammals do. Evas are not mammalian at all, even if they are human.

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilationscape » Fri Jan 25, 2008 1:41 pm Shito, I actually am more in the camp of SoLs are genderless as well, but the consensus around here as far as I knew was that he SoLs are female, as they're referred to as 'mothers'. So as far as that goes, I figured I'd run with it. And it is actually an intersting point that there aren't any particularly gender-specific details to the Evas. GNR is beyond doubt female in appearance, but whether or not she is actually female, I couldn't say for certain. I'm curious as to why Unit 01, as a clone of Lilith, shares little resembalance to its source material. One other thing I took note of: while Lilith is marshmalloy and still crucified, she seems to have semi-breastlike mounds on her chest. I guess they could be the dreaded 'moobs', though.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Fri Jan 25, 2008 3:05 pm Annihilationscape wrote: I'm curious as to why Unit 01, as a clone of Lilith, shares little resembalance to its source material.
